_target_label: FALSE — definitional exclusion (downed pilot doesn't count)
_domain_hint: Iran / Israel / Houthi / IRGC theatre. Sources: Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, BBC, IDF spokesperson, IRGC statements, Iranian state media (IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV), UKMTO maritime bulletins, ISW Iran updates.
claim: The downed F-15 pilot's involuntary presence in Iranian territory satisfies the Polymarket 'US forces enter Iran' resolution criteria.

sources.text: Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.
We have rescued the seriously wounded, and really brave, F-15 Crew Member/Officer, from deep inside the mountains of Iran. The Iranian Military was looking hard, in big numbers, and getting close.

gold.rationale_summary: The Polymarket resolution rules explicitly exclude 'pilots who are shot down' from qualifying entries. The downed F-15 pilot's presence in Iran was involuntary (his aircraft was disabled), not 'deliberate entry for operational purposes'. Therefore his presence alone does NOT satisfy resolution criteria. The market resolved YES because of the rescue forces — different US personnel who deliberately deployed for operational purposes. The pilot himself is the canonical excluded case in the rules.
required_caveats: The market did resolve YES, but on the basis of the rescue special forces, not the downed pilot himself.
This is a definitional precision test — the rules explicitly carve out involuntary entry.
